methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $15.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.8B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Applied Intuition, a Series F AI & Software company founded by Qasar Younis and Peter Ludwig, has raised $600M+ against a current post-money valuation of $15.0B. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private peers and 1 public comp (Palantir) in the AI & Software sector, spanning valuations from $3.8B to $39.0B.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.3x step-up multiplier—typical for Series F advancement—to the $15.0B last post-money valuation, implying $19.5B. Peer-median analysis across 8 same-sector, similar-valuation-tier companies yields a median of $9.2B, with selected comparables including Figure AI at $39.0B (Series C), Skild AI at $14.0B (Series C), Crusoe Energy at $10.0B (Series E), Neuralink at $9.7B, and Wayve at $8.6B (Series D).

The two methods produce a wide range, reflecting acute comparability challenges in frontier AI. Triangulation yields a low of $7.8B, a median of $14.3B, and a high of $22.4B. The current $15.0B valuation sits near the median, supported by stage-progression logic but substantially above the peer median, which reflects both the wide dispersion in early-stage AI valuations and limited direct precedent for Series F positioning in this cohort. Key uncertainties include revenue scale and retention trajectory relative to the selected peer set.
